title: 怪钟疑案
summary:
Detective Hakast (also referred to as Hercule or Inspector Hastings) interviews Mrs. Ruddock (also referred to as Mrs. Routon, Mrs. Rudd, Mrs. Luton, Mrs. Ruddle, Mrs. Rutt, and Mrs. Rotten) regarding the parents of Hila Weber. Hakast suspects that the motives for the current incident stem from the parents' past. Mrs. Ruddock reveals that Hila is the illegitimate child of her sister, Anne. Anne was a respected school teacher who became pregnant by a man named Mr. Weber, who either could not or would not marry her. To protect her career and reputation, Anne sought a clean break, giving Mrs. Ruddock half of their mother's inheritance to raise Hila. Anne subsequently moved away, possibly to Australia, to continue her teaching career, and has since had no contact with Mrs. Ruddock or Hila.

Mrs. Ruddock, a widow who previously ran a candy store in Lincolnshire before moving to the area to work for the clothing retailer "Roscoe and West," states she does not recognize a man in a photo shown by the detective. Hakast reflects on the deceased, reconsidering whether the man was a "good man" rather than the "bad man" he had previously assumed based on a false name found on a business card.

The conversation concludes with Mrs. Ruddock noting that Hila’s colleague, a girl Hakast previously encountered on the street near Cavendish Stationery, came to visit Hila but left because she had another appointment. The colleague claimed she needed to ask Hila for advice regarding an unimportant matter.
